GAME CONCEPTS

mining appropriate TNs for any task Additional rules for com bat-related actions can be found the Combat section begin- ning on p. 109 The base target number for all non combat tasks iS 10. This assumes that the task at hand is of typical difficulty for the related skill; and that the conditions surrounding the task are reasonably favorable. this is not the case, then modifiers are applied to the target number (see Modifiers, 16).

He can roll the two 6s again and does so scoring and This brings his total to 25 and there IS another 6 he can roll again: Tnis time comes Up making his final total 26. TASK RESOLUTION Sometimes the outcome character' actions will be rather automatic. For example_ there is no question that your character would be capable of walking ' down the street, picking C-bill note off the ground ad putting it in his pocket: Such simple and trivial tasks require no rules to govern them and can de assumed t0 be automatically successful. Wnen character tries t0 do something and the gamemas- ter feels that the result of the action I5 both uncertain and important t0 the game then the rules intervene t0 determine whether the action is successful or not. The gamemaster calls for an Action Check and uses the following procedure_
Attributes as Target Numbers In some situations the chances of an action' 9 success Or tailure rests mainly on the abilities of another character  The most common example of this is in a attack; where the tar- get's agility is the main factor in determining hit Or miss_ In these cases_ the two most relevant Attributes of the tar- get character are added together to produce base TN; which i5 then modified as usual. It is important t0 note that the diffi- culty modifiers would not be applied to such since diffi: culty already   factored in   by the Attributes themselves Instead_ only apply modifiers for conditions and actions see Modifiers, p: 16). For example_ ranged attack against moving character would use the target'$ INT RFL as the base target number; The target'$ Intelligence represents (in part) perceptiveness allowing a Intelligent character to notice attack and source quickly and accurately: Good Reflexes then provide the character the means tO move his body out 0f the way of the shot: is   always the gamemaster decide which Attributes are used to generate target numbers in cases where tne rules do not specify them: Always use two different Attributes when generating base target numbers from Attributes sOme cases; the first Attribute willl be obvious while tne second might take more thought; For example_ arm wrestling contest clearly involves the Strength of your opponent; Your first impulse might be t0 simply apply Strength twice_ but instead you should use Body or Willpower as the second Attribute Since represents (among other things} character' endurance, the most likely candidate would be Body:
REBOLVING ACTION CHECKS When a Action Check is called for, the gamemaster first establishes the difficulty of the task in the form of base tar- get number: Depending on the situation, certain modifiers may also apply; which may increase or decrease the target number: The task is then resolved by making 2D10 Action Check roll; Skill Check is being made the character'$ appropriate skill bonus added to the dice result: If the total result is equal to greater than the target number; the task succeeds_ If not, fails _ As for the specific results of success and failure_ see Quality of Success; P: 17 ,
Fumbles Even the best characters fail every now and then: To rep resent this any result of on the Action Check dice (both dice come up 1s) automatically fails_ regardless of the target num- ber or any modifiers_ This failure called tumble and at the gamemaster' option can have entertaining negative effects especially the character was attempting something foolhardy trivial . However, it is not advisable to penalize player too much for simple bad luck: he already failed at the Action Check; after alll
Attribute Checks Some tasks use the character' 5 Attributes rather than skills but are not resolved using another character" Attributes target: For example Mitung heawy weight simply requires STR_ or perhaps STR and BOD if the object iS especially large_ Forcing oneself to overcome tear requires WIL Check_ Such actions are called Attribute Checks and will reterred t0 in the rules as such_ For example_ when you are called upon t0 make WIL Check; it means your character must make a Attribute Check based on his WIL Attribute Likewise. Attribute Check   using BOD and STR would be  called BOD/STR Check: The base TN for an Attribute Check depends on whether single Attribute is used or two are used:
BABE TARGET NUMBER The higher the base target number (TN) assigned to task the more difficult it will be. Although sometimes an experienced gamemaster can simply assign TN and be done with it, most circumstances more detailed and precise system is called for.
